    95έΗΤ## 2.2 Component Responsibilities
    96έΗΤ
    97έΗΤ### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)
    98έΗΤ
    99έΗΤ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   100έΗΤ|----------------|----------------|
   101έΗΤ| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
   102έΗΤ| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
   103έΗΤ| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
   104έΗΤ| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
   105έΗΤ| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
   106έΗΤ| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
   107έΗΤ| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
   108έΗΤ| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
   109έΗΤ| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
   110έΗΤ| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |
   111έΗΤ
   112έΗΤ### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)
   113έΗΤ
   114έΗΤ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   115έΗΤ|----------------|----------------|
   116έΗΤ| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
   117έΗΤ| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
   118έΗΤ| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
   119έΗΤ| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
   120έΗΤ| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
   121έΗΤ| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
   122έΗΤ| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |
   123έΗΤ
   124έΗΤ### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service
   125έΗΤ
   126έΗΤ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   127έΗΤ|----------------|----------------|
   128έΗΤ| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
   129έΗΤ| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
   130έΗΤ| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
   131έΗΤ| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
   132έΗΤ| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
   133έΗΤ| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |
   134έΗΤ
   135έΗΤ### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers
   136έΗΤ
   137έΗΤ| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
   138έΗΤ|--------|----------------|-------------|
   139έΗΤ| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
   140έΗΤ| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
   141έΗΤ| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
   142έΗΤ| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
   143έΗΤ| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
   144έΗΤ| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
   145έΗΤ| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
   146έΗΤ| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |
   147έΗΤ
   148έΗΤ### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary
   149έΗΤ
   150έΗΤ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   151έΗΤ|----------------|----------------|
   152έΗΤ| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
   153έΗΤ| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
   154έΗΤ| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
   155έΗΤ| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
   156έΗΤ| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
   157έΗΤ| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |
   158έΗΤ

   160έΗΤ
   161έΗΤ## 2.3 Critical Data Flows
   162έΗΤ
   163έΗΤ### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow
   164έΗΤ
   165έΗΤ```
   166έΗΤ06:00 έΦΓ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) έΗΤ File Watcher detects
   167έΗΤ06:05 έΦΓ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
   168έΗΤ06:10 έΦΓ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
   169έΗΤ06:15 έΦΓ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
   170έΗΤ06:30 έΦΓ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
   171έΗΤ      έΦΓ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
   172έΗΤ      έΦΓ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
   173έΗΤ07:00 έΦΓ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
   174έΗΤ07:00 έΦΓ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
   175έΗΤ      έΦΓ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
   176έΗΤ      έΦΓ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
   177έΗΤ08:00 έΦΓ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
   178έΗΤ16:00 έΦΓ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
   179έΗΤ22:00 έΦΓ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
   180έΗΤ```
   181έΗΤ
   182έΗΤ### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow
   183έΗΤ
   184έΗΤ```
   185έΗΤMonday 06:00  έΦΓ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
   186έΗΤ              έΦΓ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
   187έΗΤ              έΦΓ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
   188έΗΤMonday 06:30  έΦΓ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
   189έΗΤ              έΦΓ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
   190έΗΤ              έΦΓ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
   191έΗΤMonday 07:00  έΦΓ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
   192έΗΤ              έΦΓ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
   193έΗΤ              έΦΓ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
   194έΗΤMonday 08:00  έΦΓ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
   195έΗΤTuesday       έΦΓ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
   196έΗΤ              έΦΓ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
   197έΗΤ              έΦΓ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
   198έΗΤWednesday    έΦΓ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
   199έΗΤFriday       έΦΓ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
   200έΗΤ```
   201έΗΤ
   202έΗΤ### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow
   203έΗΤ
   204έΗΤ```
   205έΗΤUser Action  έΦΓ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
   206έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
   207έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
   208έΗΤAPI Call     έΦΓ POST /api/v1/baselines
   209έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
   210έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
   211έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
   212έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
   213έΗΤPost-Publish έΦΓ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
   214έΗΤ             έΦΓ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
   215έΗΤ```
   216έΗΤ
   217έΗΤ### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow
   218έΗΤ
   219έΗΤ```
   220έΗΤUser Action  έΦΓ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
   221έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - System forks from current published baseline
   222έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - New scenario_id assigned
   223έΗΤEditing      έΦΓ Planner makes changes within scenario
   224έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
   225έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Original baseline data unchanged
   226έΗΤComparison   έΦΓ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
   227έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - System materializes both scenarios
   228έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
   229έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
   230έΗΤPromotion    έΦΓ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
   231έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
   232έΗΤ             έΦΓ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
   233έΗΤ```
   234έΗΤ

   253έΗΤ### 2.4.2 Input Message Schema
   254έΗΤ
   255έΗΤ```typescript
   256έΗΤinterface OptimizerInput {
   257έΗΤ  type: 'SOLVE_REQUEST';
   258έΗΤ  request_id: string;  // UUID for correlation
   259έΗΤ  config: {
   260έΗΤ    max_solve_time_seconds: number;  // Default: 30
   261έΗΤ    num_workers: number;             // Default: 4
   262έΗΤ    soft_constraint_penalties: {
   263έΗΤ      mileage_imbalance: number;     // Default: 100
   264έΗΤ      preference_violation: number;  // Default: 50
   265έΗΤ      overtime_hours: number;        // Default: 200
   266έΗΤ    };
   267έΗΤ    explanation_verbosity: 'MINIMAL' | 'STANDARD' | 'DETAILED';
   268έΗΤ  };
   269έΗΤ  
   270έΗΤ  trains: {
   271έΗΤ    id: string;
   272έΗΤ    train_number: string;
   273έΗΤ    fleet_id: string;
   274έΗΤ    current_mileage: number;
   275έΗΤ    target_mileage: number;  // For balancing calculation
   276έΗΤ    fitness_status: 'FIT' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'GROUNDED';
   277έΗΤ    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['NO_EXPRESS', 'MAX_SPEED_60']
   278έΗΤ    home_depot_id: string;
   279έΗΤ    current_depot_id: string;
   280έΗΤ  }[];
   281έΗΤ  
   282έΗΤ  routes: {
   283έΗΤ    id: string;
   284έΗΤ    route_code: string;
   285έΗΤ    expected_km: number;
   286έΗΤ    required_trains: number;
   287έΗΤ    allowed_fleets: string[];
   288έΗΤ    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['REQUIRES_ATO', 'LONG_PLATFORM']
   289έΗΤ  }[];
   290έΗΤ  
   291έΗΤ  // Hard constraints (must not violate)
   292έΗΤ  hard_constraints: {
   293έΗΤ    type: 'PM_DUE' | 'GROUNDED' | 'DEPOT_LOCK' | 'CERTIFICATION';
   294έΗΤ    train_id: string;
   295έΗΤ    description: string;
   296έΗΤ  }[];
   297έΗΤ  
   298έΗΤ  // Soft constraints (minimize violations)
   299έΗΤ  soft_constraints: {
   300έΗΤ    type: 'MILEAGE_BALANCE' | 'DEPOT_PREFERENCE' | 'ROUTE_PREFERENCE';
   301έΗΤ    train_id: string;
   302έΗΤ    penalty_weight: number;
   303έΗΤ    description: string;
   304έΗΤ  }[];
   305έΗΤ  
   306έΗΤ  // Material availability (NEW)
   307έΗΤ  material_positions: {
   308έΗΤ    depot_id: string;
   309έΗΤ    part_number: string;
   310έΗΤ    quantity_available: number;  // on_hand - reserved
   311έΗΤ    next_delivery_date: string | null;
   312έΗΤ  }[];
   313έΗΤ  
   314έΗΤ  // PM material requirements (NEW)
   315έΗΤ  pending_pms: {
   316έΗΤ    pm_id: string;
   317έΗΤ    train_id: string;
   318έΗΤ    pm_type: string;
   319έΗΤ    target_date: string;
   320έΗΤ    required_materials: {
   321έΗΤ      part_number: string;
   322έΗΤ      quantity: number;
   323έΗΤ      criticality: 'BLOCKING' | 'DEFERRABLE';
   324έΗΤ    }[];
   325έΗΤ  }[];
   326έΗΤ}
   327έΗΤ```
   328έΗΤ
   329έΗΤ### 2.4.3 Output Message Schema
   330έΗΤ
   331έΗΤ```typescript
   332έΗΤ// Progress updates (streamed during solve)
   333έΗΤinterface ProgressMessage {
   334έΗΤ  type: 'PROGRESS';
   335έΗΤ  request_id: string;
   336έΗΤ  percent: number;
   337έΗΤ  current_objective: number;
   338έΗΤ  feasible: boolean;
   339έΗΤ  elapsed_ms: number;
   340έΗΤ}
   341έΗΤ
   342έΗΤ// Final result
   343έΗΤinterface ResultMessage {
   344έΗΤ  type: 'RESULT';
   345έΗΤ  request_id: string;
   346έΗΤ  status: 'OPTIMAL' | 'FEASIBLE' | 'FALLBACK' | 'INFEASIBLE';
   347έΗΤ  solve_time_ms: number;
   348έΗΤ  
   349έΗΤ  assignments: {
   350έΗΤ    train_id: string;
   351έΗΤ    route_id: string;
   352έΗΤ    score: number;
   353έΗΤ    
   354έΗΤ    // NEW: Explanation trace
   355έΗΤ    explanation: {
   356έΗΤ      constraint_type: 'HARD' | 'SOFT';
   357έΗΤ      constraint_name: string;
   358έΗΤ      impact: number;  // Contribution to objective
   359έΗΤ      description: string;  // Human-readable
   360έΗΤ    }[];
   361έΗΤ  }[];
   362έΗΤ  
   363έΗΤ  // Trains not assigned (with reasons)
   364έΗΤ  unassigned: {
   365έΗΤ    train_id: string;
   366έΗΤ    reason: string;
   367έΗΤ    blocking_constraints: string[];
   368έΗΤ  }[];
   369έΗΤ  
   370έΗΤ  // Material warnings (NEW)
   371έΗΤ  material_warnings: {
   372έΗΤ    pm_id: string;
   373έΗΤ    train_id: string;
   374έΗΤ    part_number: string;
   375έΗΤ    status: 'UNAVAILABLE' | 'LOW_STOCK' | 'ARRIVING_LATE';
   376έΗΤ    description: string;
   377έΗΤ  }[];
   378έΗΤ  
   379έΗΤ  summary: {
   380έΗΤ    total_penalty: number;
   381έΗΤ    trains_assigned: number;
   382έΗΤ    trains_unassigned: number;
   383έΗΤ    soft_constraint_breakdown: Record<string, number>;
   384έΗΤ    material_blocks: number;
   385έΗΤ  };
   386έΗΤ}
   387έΗΤ
   388έΗΤ// Metrics (to stderr, OpenTelemetry format)
   389έΗΤinterface MetricsMessage {
   390έΗΤ  _type: 'metrics';
   391έΗΤ  timestamp: number;
   392έΗΤ  metrics: {
   393έΗΤ    'optimizer.solve_time_ms': number;
   394έΗΤ    'optimizer.branches_explored': number;
   395έΗΤ    'optimizer.conflicts_found': number;
   396έΗΤ    'optimizer.propagations': number;
   397έΗΤ    'optimizer.trains_assigned': number;
   398έΗΤ    'optimizer.soft_violations': number;
   399έΗΤ    'optimizer.material_blocks': number;
   400έΗΤ  };
   401έΗΤ  tags: {
   402έΗΤ    status: string;
   403έΗΤ    depot_id: string;
   404έΗΤ  };
   405έΗΤ}
   406έΗΤ
   407έΗΤ// CRITICAL: Error handling messages (addresses silent failure risk)
   408έΗΤ// See Gap Analysis GAP-003 for rationale
   409έΗΤinterface ErrorMessage {
   410έΗΤ  type: 'ERROR';
   411έΗΤ  request_id: string;
   412έΗΤ  error_code: 'SEGFAULT' | 'TIMEOUT' | 'OOM' | 'INVALID_INPUT' | 'INTERNAL_ERROR';
   413έΗΤ  error_message: string;
   414έΗΤ  stack_trace?: string;  // When available
   415έΗΤ  last_progress?: {
   416έΗΤ    percent: number;
   417έΗΤ    branches_explored: number;
   418έΗΤ    memory_mb: number;
   419έΗΤ  };
   420έΗΤ  recovery_suggestion: string;  // Human-readable
   421έΗΤ}
   422έΗΤ
   423έΗΤ// Heartbeat messages (sent every 5 seconds during solve)
   424έΗΤinterface HeartbeatMessage {
   425έΗΤ  type: 'HEARTBEAT';
   426έΗΤ  request_id: string;
   427έΗΤ  timestamp: number;
   428έΗΤ  memory_mb: number;
   429έΗΤ  thread_count: number;
   430έΗΤ  branches_per_second: number;
   431έΗΤ}
   432έΗΤ```
   433έΗΤ
   434έΗΤ### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol
   435έΗΤ
   436έΗΤ**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.
   437έΗΤ
   438έΗΤ```typescript
   439έΗΤ// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
   440έΗΤclass OptimizerWorker {
   441έΗΤ  private process: ChildProcess;
   442έΗΤ  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
   443έΗΤ  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
   444έΗΤ  
   445έΗΤ  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
   446έΗΤ  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
   447έΗΤ  
   448έΗΤ  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
   449έΗΤ    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   450έΗΤ      // Start heartbeat monitor
   451έΗΤ      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
   452έΗΤ      
   453έΗΤ      // Handle stream events
   454έΗΤ      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
   455έΗΤ        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
   456έΗΤ          if (!line.trim()) continue;
   457έΗΤ          
   458έΗΤ          try {
   459έΗΤ            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
   460έΗΤ            
   461έΗΤ            switch (msg.type) {
   462έΗΤ              case 'HEARTBEAT':
   463έΗΤ                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   464έΗΤ                break;
   465έΗΤ                
   466έΗΤ              case 'PROGRESS':
   467έΗΤ                this.emitProgress(msg);
   468έΗΤ                break;
   469έΗΤ                
   470έΗΤ              case 'RESULT':
   471έΗΤ                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   472έΗΤ                resolve(msg);
   473έΗΤ                break;
   474έΗΤ                
   475έΗΤ              case 'ERROR':
   476έΗΤ                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   477έΗΤ                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
   478έΗΤ                break;
   479έΗΤ            }
   480έΗΤ          } catch (parseError) {
   481έΗΤ            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
   482έΗΤ          }
   483έΗΤ        }
   484έΗΤ      });
   485έΗΤ      
   486έΗΤ      // Handle process crash
   487έΗΤ      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   488έΗΤ        if (code !== 0) {
   489έΗΤ          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   490έΗΤ          reject(new OptimizerError({
   491έΗΤ            type: 'ERROR',
   492έΗΤ            request_id: request.request_id,
   493έΗΤ            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
   494έΗΤ            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
   495έΗΤ            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
   496έΗΤ          }));
   497έΗΤ        }
   498έΗΤ      });
   499έΗΤ      
   500έΗΤ      // Send request
   501έΗΤ      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
   502έΗΤ    });
   503έΗΤ  }
   504έΗΤ  
   505έΗΤ  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
   506έΗΤ    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   507έΗΤ    
   508έΗΤ    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
   509έΗΤ      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
   510έΗΤ      
   511έΗΤ      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   512έΗΤ        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   513έΗΤ        
   514έΗΤ        // Kill hung process
   515έΗΤ        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
   516έΗΤ        
   517έΗΤ        reject(new OptimizerError({
   518έΗΤ          type: 'ERROR',
   519έΗΤ          request_id: requestId,
   520έΗΤ          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
   521έΗΤ          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
   522έΗΤ          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
   523έΗΤ        }));
   524έΗΤ      }
   525έΗΤ    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
   526έΗΤ  }
   527έΗΤ  
   528έΗΤ  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
   529έΗΤ    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
   530έΗΤ      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
   531έΗΤ      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
   532έΗΤ    }
   533έΗΤ  }
   534έΗΤ}
   535έΗΤ
   536έΗΤclass OptimizerError extends Error {
   537έΗΤ  readonly errorCode: string;
   538έΗΤ  readonly requestId: string;
   539έΗΤ  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
   540έΗΤ  readonly stackTrace?: string;
   541έΗΤ  
   542έΗΤ  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
   543έΗΤ    super(msg.error_message);
   544έΗΤ    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
   545έΗΤ    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
   546έΗΤ    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
   547έΗΤ    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
   548έΗΤ    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
   549έΗΤ  }
   550έΗΤ}
   551έΗΤ```
   552έΗΤ
   553έΗΤ**C++ Implementation Notes:**
   554έΗΤ
   555έΗΤThe C++ binary must:
   556έΗΤ1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
   557έΗΤ2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
   558έΗΤ3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
   559έΗΤ4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)
   560έΗΤ
   561έΗΤ```cpp
   562έΗΤ// Signal handler example (C++)
   563έΗΤvoid signalHandler(int signal) {
   564έΗΤ    nlohmann::json error;
   565έΗΤ    error["type"] = "ERROR";
   566έΗΤ    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
   567έΗΤ    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
   568έΗΤ    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
   569έΗΤ    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
   570έΗΤ    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
   571έΗΤ    
   572έΗΤ    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
   573έΗΤ    std::cout.flush();
   574έΗΤ    
   575έΗΤ    _exit(128 + signal);
   576έΗΤ}
   577έΗΤ```
   578έΗΤ
   579έΗΤThe Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.
   580έΗΤ
   581έΗΤ### 2.5.1 Purpose
   582έΗΤ
   583έΗΤThe Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.
   584έΗΤ
   585έΗΤ### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process
   586έΗΤ
   587έΗΤ```typescript
   588έΗΤinterface ConstraintEnvelope {
   589έΗΤ  period: {
   590έΗΤ    start: Date;  // Quarter start
   591έΗΤ    end: Date;    // Quarter end
   592έΗΤ  };
   593έΗΤ  
   594έΗΤ  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
   595έΗΤ  track_capacity: {
   596έΗΤ    depot_id: string;
   597έΗΤ    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
   598έΗΤ    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
   599έΗΤ    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
   600έΗΤ    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
   601έΗΤ  }[];
   602έΗΤ  
   603έΗΤ  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
   604έΗΤ  labor_capacity: {
   605έΗΤ    depot_id: string;
   606έΗΤ    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
   607έΗΤ    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
   608έΗΤ    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
   609έΗΤ    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
   610έΗΤ  }[];
   611έΗΤ  
   612έΗΤ  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
   613έΗΤ  material_capacity: {
   614έΗΤ    depot_id: string;
   615έΗΤ    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
   616έΗΤ    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
   617έΗΤ    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
   618έΗΤ    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
   619έΗΤ  }[];
   620έΗΤ  
   621έΗΤ  // Constraints from tactical model
   622έΗΤ  blocked_trains: {
   623έΗΤ    train_id: string;
   624έΗΤ    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
   625έΗΤ    reason: string;
   626έΗΤ  }[];
   627έΗΤ}
   628έΗΤ
   629έΗΤclass ConstraintAggregationService {
   630έΗΤ  /**
   631έΗΤ   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   632έΗΤ   */
   633έΗΤ  async generateEnvelope(
   634έΗΤ    quarterStart: Date,
   635έΗΤ    quarterEnd: Date,
   636έΗΤ    depotIds: string[]
   637έΗΤ  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
   638έΗΤ    // 1. Aggregate track availability
   639έΗΤ    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
   640έΗΤ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   641έΗΤ    );
   642έΗΤ    
   643έΗΤ    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
   644έΗΤ    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
   645έΗΤ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   646έΗΤ    );
   647έΗΤ    
   648έΗΤ    // 3. Aggregate material projections
   649έΗΤ    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
   650έΗΤ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   651έΗΤ    );
   652έΗΤ    
   653έΗΤ    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
   654έΗΤ    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
   655έΗΤ      quarterStart, quarterEnd
   656έΗΤ    );
   657έΗΤ    
   658έΗΤ    return {
   659έΗΤ      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
   660έΗΤ      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
   661έΗΤ      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
   662έΗΤ      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
   663έΗΤ      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
   664έΗΤ    };
   665έΗΤ  }
   666έΗΤ  
   667έΗΤ  /**
   668έΗΤ   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   669έΗΤ   */
   670έΗΤ  async validateStrategicAllocation(
   671έΗΤ    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
   672έΗΤ    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
   673έΗΤ  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
   674έΗΤ    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
   675έΗΤ    
   676έΗΤ    // Check track capacity
   677έΗΤ    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
   678έΗΤ      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
   679έΗΤ        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
   680έΗΤ             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
   681έΗΤ      );
   682έΗΤ      
   683έΗΤ      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
   684έΗΤ        violations.push({
   685έΗΤ          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
   686έΗΤ          severity: 'HARD',
   687έΗΤ          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
   688έΗΤ        });
   689έΗΤ      }
   690έΗΤ    }
   691έΗΤ    
   692έΗΤ    // Check labor capacity
   693έΗΤ    // Check material capacity
   694έΗΤ    // ... similar validation logic
   695έΗΤ    
   696έΗΤ    return {
   697έΗΤ      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
   698έΗΤ      violations
   699έΗΤ    };
   700έΗΤ  }
   701έΗΤ}
   702έΗΤ```
   703έΗΤ
   704έΗΤ---
   705έΗΤ
   706έΗΤ## 2.6 Key Design Principles
   707έΗΤ
   708έΗΤ### 2.6.1 Explainability First
   709έΗΤ
   710έΗΤEvery optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:
   711έΗΤ
   712έΗΤ- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
   713έΗΤ- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
   714έΗΤ- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
   715έΗΤ- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used
   716έΗΤ
   717έΗΤ### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation
   718έΗΤ
   719έΗΤThe system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:
   720έΗΤ
   721έΗΤ- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
   722έΗΤ- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
   723έΗΤ- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
   724έΗΤ- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records
   725έΗΤ
   726έΗΤ### 2.6.3 Audit Everything
   727έΗΤ
   728έΗΤFor 12-year government contract compliance:
   729έΗΤ
   730έΗΤ- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
   731έΗΤ- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
   732έΗΤ- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
   733έΗΤ- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved
   734έΗΤ
   735έΗΤ### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning
   736έΗΤ
   737έΗΤPrevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:
   738έΗΤ
   739έΗΤ- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
   740έΗΤ- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
   741έΗΤ- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
   742έΗΤ- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
   743έΗΤ

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 2: System Architecture

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 2.1 High-Level Architecture

## 2.2 Component Responsibilities

### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |

### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |

### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |

### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers

| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
|--------|----------------|-------------|
| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |

### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |

## 2.3 Critical Data Flows

### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow

```
06:00 έΦΓ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) έΗΤ File Watcher detects
06:05 έΦΓ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
06:10 έΦΓ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
06:15 έΦΓ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
06:30 έΦΓ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
      έΦΓ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
      έΦΓ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
07:00 έΦΓ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
07:00 έΦΓ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
      έΦΓ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
      έΦΓ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
08:00 έΦΓ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
16:00 έΦΓ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
22:00 έΦΓ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
```

### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow

```
Monday 06:00  έΦΓ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
              έΦΓ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
              έΦΓ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
Monday 06:30  έΦΓ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
              έΦΓ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
              έΦΓ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
Monday 07:00  έΦΓ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
              έΦΓ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
              έΦΓ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
Monday 08:00  έΦΓ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
Tuesday       έΦΓ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
              έΦΓ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
              έΦΓ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
Wednesday    έΦΓ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
Friday       έΦΓ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
```

### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow

```
User Action  έΦΓ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
             έΦΓ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
             έΦΓ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
API Call     έΦΓ POST /api/v1/baselines
             έΦΓ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
             έΦΓ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
             έΦΓ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
             έΦΓ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
Post-Publish έΦΓ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
             έΦΓ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
```

### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow

```
User Action  έΦΓ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
             έΦΓ   - System forks from current published baseline
             έΦΓ   - New scenario_id assigned
Editing      έΦΓ Planner makes changes within scenario
             έΦΓ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
             έΦΓ   - Original baseline data unchanged
Comparison   έΦΓ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
             έΦΓ   - System materializes both scenarios
             έΦΓ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
             έΦΓ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
Promotion    έΦΓ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
             έΦΓ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
             έΦΓ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
```

### 2.4.2 Input Message Schema

```typescript
interface OptimizerInput {
  type: 'SOLVE_REQUEST';
  request_id: string;  // UUID for correlation
  config: {
    max_solve_time_seconds: number;  // Default: 30
    num_workers: number;             // Default: 4
    soft_constraint_penalties: {
      mileage_imbalance: number;     // Default: 100
      preference_violation: number;  // Default: 50
      overtime_hours: number;        // Default: 200
    };
    explanation_verbosity: 'MINIMAL' | 'STANDARD' | 'DETAILED';
  };
  
  trains: {
    id: string;
    train_number: string;
    fleet_id: string;
    current_mileage: number;
    target_mileage: number;  // For balancing calculation
    fitness_status: 'FIT' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'GROUNDED';
    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['NO_EXPRESS', 'MAX_SPEED_60']
    home_depot_id: string;
    current_depot_id: string;
  }[];
  
  routes: {
    id: string;
    route_code: string;
    expected_km: number;
    required_trains: number;
    allowed_fleets: string[];
    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['REQUIRES_ATO', 'LONG_PLATFORM']
  }[];
  
  // Hard constraints (must not violate)
  hard_constraints: {
    type: 'PM_DUE' | 'GROUNDED' | 'DEPOT_LOCK' | 'CERTIFICATION';
    train_id: string;
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  // Soft constraints (minimize violations)
  soft_constraints: {
    type: 'MILEAGE_BALANCE' | 'DEPOT_PREFERENCE' | 'ROUTE_PREFERENCE';
    train_id: string;
    penalty_weight: number;
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  // Material availability (NEW)
  material_positions: {
    depot_id: string;
    part_number: string;
    quantity_available: number;  // on_hand - reserved
    next_delivery_date: string | null;
  }[];
  
  // PM material requirements (NEW)
  pending_pms: {
    pm_id: string;
    train_id: string;
    pm_type: string;
    target_date: string;
    required_materials: {
      part_number: string;
      quantity: number;
      criticality: 'BLOCKING' | 'DEFERRABLE';
    }[];
  }[];
}
```

### 2.4.3 Output Message Schema

```typescript
// Progress updates (streamed during solve)
interface ProgressMessage {
  type: 'PROGRESS';
  request_id: string;
  percent: number;
  current_objective: number;
  feasible: boolean;
  elapsed_ms: number;
}

// Final result
interface ResultMessage {
  type: 'RESULT';
  request_id: string;
  status: 'OPTIMAL' | 'FEASIBLE' | 'FALLBACK' | 'INFEASIBLE';
  solve_time_ms: number;
  
  assignments: {
    train_id: string;
    route_id: string;
    score: number;
    
    // NEW: Explanation trace
    explanation: {
      constraint_type: 'HARD' | 'SOFT';
      constraint_name: string;
      impact: number;  // Contribution to objective
      description: string;  // Human-readable
    }[];
  }[];
  
  // Trains not assigned (with reasons)
  unassigned: {
    train_id: string;
    reason: string;
    blocking_constraints: string[];
  }[];
  
  // Material warnings (NEW)
  material_warnings: {
    pm_id: string;
    train_id: string;
    part_number: string;
    status: 'UNAVAILABLE' | 'LOW_STOCK' | 'ARRIVING_LATE';
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  summary: {
    total_penalty: number;
    trains_assigned: number;
    trains_unassigned: number;
    soft_constraint_breakdown: Record<string, number>;
    material_blocks: number;
  };
}

// Metrics (to stderr, OpenTelemetry format)
interface MetricsMessage {
  _type: 'metrics';
  timestamp: number;
  metrics: {
    'optimizer.solve_time_ms': number;
    'optimizer.branches_explored': number;
    'optimizer.conflicts_found': number;
    'optimizer.propagations': number;
    'optimizer.trains_assigned': number;
    'optimizer.soft_violations': number;
    'optimizer.material_blocks': number;
  };
  tags: {
    status: string;
    depot_id: string;
  };
}

// CRITICAL: Error handling messages (addresses silent failure risk)
// See Gap Analysis GAP-003 for rationale
interface ErrorMessage {
  type: 'ERROR';
  request_id: string;
  error_code: 'SEGFAULT' | 'TIMEOUT' | 'OOM' | 'INVALID_INPUT' | 'INTERNAL_ERROR';
  error_message: string;
  stack_trace?: string;  // When available
  last_progress?: {
    percent: number;
    branches_explored: number;
    memory_mb: number;
  };
  recovery_suggestion: string;  // Human-readable
}

// Heartbeat messages (sent every 5 seconds during solve)
interface HeartbeatMessage {
  type: 'HEARTBEAT';
  request_id: string;
  timestamp: number;
  memory_mb: number;
  thread_count: number;
  branches_per_second: number;
}
```

### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol

**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.

```typescript
// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
class OptimizerWorker {
  private process: ChildProcess;
  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
  
  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
  
  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      // Start heartbeat monitor
      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
      
      // Handle stream events
      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
          if (!line.trim()) continue;
          
          try {
            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
            
            switch (msg.type) {
              case 'HEARTBEAT':
                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
                break;
                
              case 'PROGRESS':
                this.emitProgress(msg);
                break;
                
              case 'RESULT':
                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
                resolve(msg);
                break;
                
              case 'ERROR':
                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
                break;
            }
          } catch (parseError) {
            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
          }
        }
      });
      
      // Handle process crash
      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
        if (code !== 0) {
          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
          reject(new OptimizerError({
            type: 'ERROR',
            request_id: request.request_id,
            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
          }));
        }
      });
      
      // Send request
      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
    });
  }
  
  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
    
    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
      
      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
        
        // Kill hung process
        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
        
        reject(new OptimizerError({
          type: 'ERROR',
          request_id: requestId,
          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
        }));
      }
    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
  }
  
  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
    }
  }
}

class OptimizerError extends Error {
  readonly errorCode: string;
  readonly requestId: string;
  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
  readonly stackTrace?: string;
  
  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
    super(msg.error_message);
    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
  }
}
```

**C++ Implementation Notes:**

The C++ binary must:
1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)

```cpp
// Signal handler example (C++)
void signalHandler(int signal) {
    nlohmann::json error;
    error["type"] = "ERROR";
    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
    
    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
    std::cout.flush();
    
    _exit(128 + signal);
}
```

The Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.

### 2.5.1 Purpose

The Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.

### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process

```typescript
interface ConstraintEnvelope {
  period: {
    start: Date;  // Quarter start
    end: Date;    // Quarter end
  };
  
  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
  track_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
  }[];
  
  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
  labor_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
  }[];
  
  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
  material_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
  }[];
  
  // Constraints from tactical model
  blocked_trains: {
    train_id: string;
    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
    reason: string;
  }[];
}

class ConstraintAggregationService {
  /**
   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   */
  async generateEnvelope(
    quarterStart: Date,
    quarterEnd: Date,
    depotIds: string[]
  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
    // 1. Aggregate track availability
    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 3. Aggregate material projections
    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd
    );
    
    return {
      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
    };
  }
  
  /**
   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   */
  async validateStrategicAllocation(
    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
    
    // Check track capacity
    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
      );
      
      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
        violations.push({
          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
          severity: 'HARD',
          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
        });
      }
    }
    
    // Check labor capacity
    // Check material capacity
    // ... similar validation logic
    
    return {
      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
      violations
    };
  }
}
```

---

## 2.6 Key Design Principles

### 2.6.1 Explainability First

Every optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:

- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used

### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation

The system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:

- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records

### 2.6.3 Audit Everything

For 12-year government contract compliance:

- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved

### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning

Prevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:

- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs

   171έΗΤ### 1.3 @belt/tools Migration (P1 - Critical)
   172έΗΤ
   173έΗΤ> **CRITICAL**: Project uses Zod for validation but README mandates @belt/tools.
   174έΗΤ> zustand is in package.json but BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh
   175έΗΤ> React hooks (useState, useEffect, etc.) are BLOCKED by .agentrc.ts - use machine.useMachine()
   176έΗΤ
   177έΗΤ#### Schema Migration (Zod έΗΤ @belt/tools)
   178έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Migrate primitive schemas (uuidSchema, dateStringSchema, mileageKmSchema) to createType
   179έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Migrate enum schemas (fitnessStatusSchema, pmStatusSchema, dayTypeSchema, etc.) to createType enum syntax
   180έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Migrate object schemas (trainSchema, pmScheduleSchema, mileageReadingSchema) to createSchema
   181έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Migrate CSV row schemas (mileageCsvRowSchema, fleetRosterCsvRowSchema) with transform handling
   182έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Migrate optimizer IPC schemas (optimizerInputSchema, optimizerOutputSchema)
   183έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Migrate API response schemas (apiSuccessSchema, apiErrorSchema)
   184έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Update parseSchema helper to use @belt/tools parse method
   185έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Update formatZodErrors to work with @belt/tools validation errors
   186έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Update all service imports from 'zod' to '@belt/tools'
   187έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Remove 'zod' from package.json dependencies
   188έΗΤ- [ ] P1: Remove 'zustand' from package.json dependencies (FORBIDDEN by hooks)
   189έΗΤ
   190έΗΤ#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI)
   191έΗΤ- [ ] P2: Create src/store/index.ts with @belt/tools createMachine
   192έΗΤ- [ ] P2: Create fleetSlice (trains, selectedTrain, fitnessStatus filters)
   193έΗΤ- [ ] P2: Create canvasSlice (zoom, pan, viewportBounds, activeLayer)
   194έΗΤ- [ ] P2: Create pmSlice (schedules, selectedPM, dragState, constraints)
   195έΗΤ- [ ] P2: Create batch dispatch utility for 60fps WebGL updates (requestAnimationFrame batching)
   196έΗΤ
   197έΗΤ#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
   198έΗΤ```typescript
   199έΗΤ// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
   200έΗΤimport { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';
   201έΗΤ
   202έΗΤconst TrainModel = createSchema({
   203έΗΤ  id: 'string',
   204έΗΤ  trainNumber: 'string',
   205έΗΤ  mileageKm: 'number',
   206έΗΤ  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
   207έΗΤ  tags: 'string[]',
   208έΗΤ  metadata: 'object?',
   209έΗΤ});
   210έΗΤtype Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
   211έΗΤconst train = TrainModel.parse(data);
   212έΗΤ
   213έΗΤ// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
   214έΗΤimport { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';
   215έΗΤ
   216έΗΤconst fleetSlice = createSlice({
   217έΗΤ  id: 'fleet',
   218έΗΤ  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
   219έΗΤ  cache: { policy: 'session' },
   220έΗΤ  reducers: {
   221έΗΤ    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
   222έΗΤ    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
   223έΗΤ  },
   224έΗΤ});
   225έΗΤ
   226έΗΤconst machine = createMachine({
   227έΗΤ  id: 'mms',
   228έΗΤ  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
   229έΗΤ  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
   230έΗΤ});
   231έΗΤ
   232έΗΤ// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
   233έΗΤconst { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
   234έΗΤ```

  1072έΗΤ| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Material constraint validation | Assume unlimited (warning flag) |

  1083έΗΤ4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter

# MMS Implementation Plan

> **Status**: Comprehensive plan generated from spec analysis (v2.1)
> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-19
> **Priority**: P1 (Critical) έΗΤ P2 (High) έΗΤ P3 (Medium) έΗΤ P4 (Low)

---

## Current State Analysis

**Codebase Status**: Database Layer Complete (Phase 1.1)
- React 18 + Vite frontend shell (App.tsx, main.tsx)
- **Database schema**: 30+ tables across 14 schema files (Drizzle ORM)
  - Core Fleet: depots, fleets, trains, routes, tracks
  - Operational Constraints: service_demand, route_utilization_profiles, unavailability_events, train_route_compatibility
  - PM Management: pm_types, pm_material_requirements, pm_schedule
  - Mileage Tracking: mileage_readings, meter_events
  - Component Tracking: train_components, component_installations, component_pm_tracking
  - Material Management: inventory_positions, material_reservations
  - Man-hour Planning: manhour_capacity, manhour_projections, manhour_adjustments
  - Baseline & Drift: plan_baselines, baseline_pm_schedule, baseline_mileage_projection, drift_analysis
  - Scenarios: scenarios, scenario_deltas, scenario_comparisons
  - Costing: labor_rates, material_costs, cost_baselines
  - Audit & Auth: users, roles, sessions, pm_schedule_history, audit_log
- **Database utilities**: Connection pool with health checks (src/lib/db/connection.ts)
- **Drizzle config**: Migration configuration (drizzle.config.ts)
- **Manual migrations** (drizzle/manual/):
  - PostgreSQL extensions (uuid-ossp, timescaledb)
  - TimescaleDB hypertable (7-day chunks), compression (30 days), retention (13 years)
  - daily_fleet_mileage continuous aggregate
  - Database functions: train fitness sync, canvas refresh, scenario cleanup, reservation release, fleet targets, PM hierarchy, audit
  - Canvas views: v_canvas_strategic, v_canvas_tactical, v_manhour_canvas, v_drift_canvas
  - Development seed data
- Dependencies: drizzle-orm, postgres.js, pino, @belt/tools

**Next Priority**: Core Domain Services (Ingestion, Unavailability, Service Demand, Fleet Service)

**Critical Path**: ~~Database Schema~~ έΗΤ ~~TimescaleDB Config~~ έΗΤ ~~Shared Lib~~ έΗΤ Domain Services έΗΤ Optimizer έΗΤ API έΗΤ UI

---

## Phase 1: Foundation (P1 - Critical)

### 1.1 Database Schema & Migrations

#### Core Fleet Domain
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `depots` table (code, name, location, timezone)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `fleets` table (code, name, manufacturer, cars_per_train, base_depot_id)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `trains` table (train_number, fleet_id, current_mileage_km, fitness_status, target_mileage_km, display_color)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `routes` table (route_code, expected_km_per_day, required_trains, allowed_fleet_ids, signaling_system, power_system)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `tracks` table (depot_id, track_number, track_type, max_train_length_m, capabilities)

#### Operational Constraints Domain (v2.1 Critical)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `service_demand` table (fleet_id, day_type, min_trains_required, peak requirements)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `route_utilization_profiles` table (route_id, weekday_km, weekend_km, confidence)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `unavailability_events` table (train_id, event_type, status, restriction_level, estimated_return_date, source_system)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `train_route_compatibility` table (train_id/fleet_id, route_id, is_compatible, constraint_type)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `track_capabilities` table (track_id, capability, is_operational)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_capability_requirements` table (pm_type_id, capability, is_mandatory)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_baseline_assumptions` table (train_id, pm_type_id, assumption_type, confidence_level) - cold start handling

#### PM Management Domain
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_types` table (fleet_id, pm_code, interval_km, interval_months, satisfies_codes[], estimated_hours, track_type)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_material_requirements` table (pm_type_id, part_number, quantity_per_pm, criticality, lead_time_days)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_schedule` table (train_id, pm_type_id, target_date, status, constraint_start/end, assigned_track_id, is_locked, scenario_id)

#### Mileage Tracking (TimescaleDB)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `mileage_readings` hypertable (train_id, reading_date, raw_reading, adjusted_reading, daily_km, validation_status)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `meter_events` table (train_id, event_date, event_type, old_reading, new_reading, adjustment_km)
- [x] P1: Configure TimescaleDB hypertable with 7-day chunks
- [x] P1: Configure compression policy (30 days)
- [x] P1: Configure retention policy (13 years)
- [x] P1: Create `daily_fleet_mileage` continuous aggregate view

#### Component Tracking
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `train_components` table (component_type, serial_number, current_train_id, component_mileage_km, status)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `component_installations` table (component_id, train_id, installed_date, removed_date)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `component_pm_tracking` table (component_id, pm_type_id, last_pm_date, next_pm_due)

#### Material Management
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `inventory_positions` table (depot_id, part_number, quantity_on_hand, quantity_reserved, quantity_available)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `material_reservations` table (pm_schedule_id, inventory_position_id, quantity_reserved, reservation_type)

#### Man-hour Planning
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `manhour_capacity` table (depot_id, day_shift_hours, night_shift_hours, technicians)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `manhour_projections` table (depot_id, period_start, required_hours, available_hours, utilization_pct)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `manhour_adjustments` table (projection_id, adjustment_type, hours_adjusted, status)

#### Baseline & Drift Domain
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `plan_baselines` table (name, version, baseline_date, status, pm metrics)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `baseline_pm_schedule` table (baseline_id, train_id, pm_type_id, projected_date)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `baseline_mileage_projection` table (baseline_id, train_id, projection_date, projected_cumulative_km)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `drift_analysis` table (baseline_id, train_id, mileage_drift_km, drift_pct, requires_replan)

#### Scenario Management (Event-Sourced)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `scenarios` table (name, forked_from_baseline_id, status, expires_at)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `scenario_deltas` table (scenario_id, sequence_num, delta_type, entity_id, old_values, new_values)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `scenario_comparisons` table (scenario_a_id, scenario_b_id, baseline_id, metrics)

#### Costing Domain
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `labor_rates` table (depot_id, regular_hourly_rate, overtime_multiplier, shop_rate)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `material_costs` table (part_number, unit_cost, effective_from)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `cost_baselines` table (fiscal_year, depot_id, budget vs. actual)

#### Audit & Auth Domain
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_schedule_history` table (pm_schedule_id, change_type, old/new values, changed_by)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `audit_log` table (entity_type, entity_id, action, user_id, old/new values)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `users` table (email, password_hash, role_ids, primary_depot_id)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `roles` table (name, permissions JSONB)
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `sessions` table (user_id, token_hash, expires_at)

#### Enums & Types
- [x] P1: Create PostgreSQL enums: fitness_status, pm_status, track_type, shift_type, adjustment_status, baseline_status, scenario_status, material_criticality, reservation_type, day_type, unavailability_type, unavailability_status, track_capability, constraint_type, assumption_type, confidence_level

#### Database Functions & Triggers
- [x] P1: Create trigger `sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()` - auto-update train fitness on unavailability events
- [x] P1: Create function `refresh_canvas_views()` - concurrent materialized view refresh
- [x] P1: Create function `cleanup_expired_scenarios()` - scenario expiration
- [x] P1: Create function `release_expired_reservations()` - soft reservation cleanup
- [x] P1: Create function `update_fleet_mileage_targets()` - fleet balancing recalculation

#### Materialized Views (Canvas Optimization)
- [x] P1: Create materialized view `v_canvas_strategic` (yearly PM aggregates per train)
- [x] P1: Create materialized view `v_canvas_tactical` (weekly PM details)
- [x] P1: Create view `v_manhour_canvas` (man-hour overlay data)
- [x] P1: Create view `v_drift_canvas` (drift severity overlay)

#### Migrations
- [x] P1: Write migration for PostgreSQL extensions (uuid-ossp, timescaledb)
- [ ] P1: Write migration runner with rollback support
- [x] P1: Create seed data script for development
- [ ] P1: Run migrations and verify schema

### 1.2 Shared Library (src/lib/)

#### Database Utilities
- [x] P1: Create Drizzle database connection pool with health checks
- [x] P1: Create PostgreSQL connection configuration (max 20 connections)
- [x] P1: Create Redis connection utilities for cache/sessions/BullMQ
- [x] P1: Create transaction helper with automatic rollback

#### Base Service Infrastructure
- [x] P1: Create base service class with OpenTelemetry tracing integration
- [x] P1: Create Pino structured logging configuration (JSON format, sensitive field redaction)
- [x] P1: Create prom-client Prometheus metrics registry
- [x] P1: Create HTTP metrics (requests_total, request_duration_seconds histogram)
- [x] P1: Create business metrics (pm_scheduled_total, optimizer_solve_seconds, fleet_mileage_km, drift_trains_requiring_replan)

#### Queue Infrastructure
- [x] P1: Create BullMQ queue configuration (connection, default options)
- [x] P1: Create typed queue definitions for all 8 worker types
- [x] P1: Create job progress streaming utilities

#### Validation Utilities
- [ ] P1: Migrate Zod schemas to @belt/tools createSchema/createType (see Phase 1.3)
- [ ] P1: Update CSV import validation to use @belt/tools
- [ ] P1: Update optimizer IPC schemas to use @belt/tools

#### Date/Time Utilities
- [x] P1: Create compliance window calculation utilities (┬▒10% tolerance)
- [x] P1: Create PM hierarchy resolution utilities (C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3)
- [x] P1: Create mileage projection utilities (velocity-based + time-based triggers)
- [x] P1: Create timezone-aware date utilities

#### Error Handling
- [x] P1: Create typed error classes (ValidationError, NotFoundError, ConflictError, MaximoLockError, etc.)
- [x] P1: Create error code registry (FILE_001, ROW_001, SYS_001, etc.)
- [x] P1: Create retry configuration utilities (exponential backoff)
- [x] P1: Create circuit breaker implementation

### 1.3 @belt/tools Migration (P1 - Critical)

> **CRITICAL**: Project uses Zod for validation but README mandates @belt/tools.
> zustand is in package.json but BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh
> React hooks (useState, useEffect, etc.) are BLOCKED by .agentrc.ts - use machine.useMachine()

#### Schema Migration (Zod έΗΤ @belt/tools)
- [ ] P1: Migrate primitive schemas (uuidSchema, dateStringSchema, mileageKmSchema) to createType

#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI)
- [ ] P2: Create src/store/index.ts with @belt/tools createMachine

#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
```typescript
// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
  tags: 'string[]',
  metadata: 'object?',
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);

// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

- [ ] **MEDIUM**: TypeScript strict mode is partially disabled (noImplicitAny: false) - should be enabled

## Critical Dependencies

| Dependency | Source | Required For | Fallback |
|-----------|--------|--------------|----------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | PM scheduling validity | Manual status UI |
| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Material constraint validation | Assume unlimited (warning flag) |
| Service demand | Operations input | Run assignment optimization | Default 85% fleet |
| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Mileage projections | Fleet-wide average (warning flag) |

---

## Notes

1. **Non-negotiable dependencies**: Corrective maintenance visibility and service demand are prerequisites for valid schedules
2. **Dual-solver architecture**: Strategic (GA) and tactical (OR-Tools CP-SAT) must share constraint envelope
3. **Explainability**: Every optimizer decision must include factor-based explanation traces
4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter
5. **Material constraints**: PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless
6. **Cold-start risk**: Historical PM data will be incomplete - explicit confidence scoring required
7. **Audit compliance**: 12-year government contract mandates immutable audit trail with full attribution

    88έΗΤ## 8.2 Container Images
    89έΗΤ
    90έΗΤ### 8.2.1 Image Registry
    91έΗΤ
    92έΗΤ```yaml
    93έΗΤRegistry: registry.metro.example/mms
    94έΗΤNaming: mms-<component>:<version>
    95έΗΤTagging:
    96έΗΤ  - latest (dev only)
    97έΗΤ  - <semver> (e.g., 2.1.0)
    98έΗΤ  - <semver>-<commit> (e.g., 2.1.0-abc1234)
    99έΗΤ```
   100έΗΤ
   101έΗΤ### 8.2.2 Container Images
   102έΗΤ
   103έΗΤ| Image | Base | Size Target | Purpose |
   104έΗΤ|-------|------|-------------|---------|
   105έΗΤ| `mms-frontend` | nginx:alpine | <50MB | Static React assets |
   106έΗΤ| `mms-backend` | oven/bun:alpine | <200MB | API server |
   107έΗΤ| `mms-worker` | oven/bun:alpine | <200MB | Background workers |
   108έΗΤ| `mms-optimizer` | debian:slim | <500MB | C++ optimizer binary |
   109έΗΤ| `mms-file-watcher` | oven/bun:alpine | <150MB | File ingestion daemon |
   110έΗΤ
   111έΗΤ### 8.2.3 Dockerfile Examples
   112έΗΤ
   113έΗΤ**Backend Dockerfile:**
   114έΗΤ```dockerfile
   115έΗΤ# Build stage
   116έΗΤFROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine AS builder
   117έΗΤ
   118έΗΤWORKDIR /app
   119έΗΤCOPY package.json bun.lockb ./
   120έΗΤRUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
   121έΗΤ
   122έΗΤCOPY . .
   123έΗΤRUN bun run build
   124έΗΤ
   125έΗΤ# Production stage
   126έΗΤFROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine
   127έΗΤ
   128έΗΤWORKDIR /app
   129έΗΤ
   130έΗΤ# Add non-root user
   131έΗΤRUN addgroup -S mms && adduser -S mms -G mms
   132έΗΤUSER mms
   133έΗΤ
   134έΗΤCOPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
   135έΗΤCOPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
   136έΗΤCOPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./
   137έΗΤ
   138έΗΤENV NODE_ENV=production
   139έΗΤENV PORT=8000
   140έΗΤ
   141έΗΤEXPOSE 8000
   142έΗΤ
   143έΗΤHEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
   144έΗΤ  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8000/health || exit 1
   145έΗΤ
   146έΗΤCMD ["bun", "run", "dist/server.js"]
   147έΗΤ```
   148έΗΤ
   149έΗΤ**Optimizer Dockerfile:**
   150έΗΤ```dockerfile
   151έΗΤ# Build stage
   152έΗΤFROM debian:bookworm-slim AS builder
   153έΗΤ
   154έΗΤRUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   155έΗΤ  build-essential \
   156έΗΤ  cmake \
   157έΗΤ  git \
   158έΗΤ  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
   159έΗΤ
   160έΗΤ# Install OR-Tools
   161έΗΤRUN git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/google/or-tools.git /or-tools
   162έΗΤWORKDIR /or-tools
   163έΗΤRUN cmake -S . -B build -DBUILD_DEPS=ON
   164έΗΤRUN cmake --build build --config Release -j$(nproc)
   165έΗΤ
   166έΗΤ# Build optimizer
   167έΗΤWORKDIR /app
   168έΗΤCOPY . .
   169έΗΤRUN cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=/or-tools/build
   170έΗΤRUN cmake --build build --config Release
   171έΗΤ
   172έΗΤ# Production stage
   173έΗΤFROM debian:bookworm-slim
   174έΗΤ
   175έΗΤRUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   176έΗΤ  libstdc++6 \
   177έΗΤ  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
   178έΗΤ
   179έΗΤ# Add non-root user
   180έΗΤRUN useradd -r -s /bin/false mms
   181έΗΤUSER mms
   182έΗΤ
   183έΗΤWORKDIR /app
   184έΗΤCOPY --from=builder /app/build/mms-optimizer .
   185έΗΤ
   186έΗΤENTRYPOINT ["./mms-optimizer"]
   187έΗΤ```
   188έΗΤ
   189έΗΤ---

   191έΗΤ## 8.3 Kubernetes Manifests

   199έΗΤ  name: mms-backend
   200έΗΤ  namespace: mms

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 8: Operations & Deployment

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 8.1 Deployment Architecture

### 8.1.1 Environment Overview

| Environment | Purpose | Infrastructure | Data |
|-------------|---------|----------------|------|
| Development | Feature development | Docker Compose (local) | Synthetic |
| Staging | Integration testing | Kubernetes (single node) | Anonymized prod copy |
| UAT | User acceptance | Kubernetes (3 nodes) | Anonymized prod copy |
| Production | Live system | Kubernetes (5+ nodes) | Live data |

## 8.2 Container Images

### 8.2.1 Image Registry

```yaml
Registry: registry.metro.example/mms
Naming: mms-<component>:<version>
Tagging:
  - latest (dev only)
  - <semver> (e.g., 2.1.0)
  - <semver>-<commit> (e.g., 2.1.0-abc1234)
```

### 8.2.2 Container Images

| Image | Base | Size Target | Purpose |
|-------|------|-------------|---------|
| `mms-frontend` | nginx:alpine | <50MB | Static React assets |
| `mms-backend` | oven/bun:alpine | <200MB | API server |
| `mms-worker` | oven/bun:alpine | <200MB | Background workers |
| `mms-optimizer` | debian:slim | <500MB | C++ optimizer binary |
| `mms-file-watcher` | oven/bun:alpine | <150MB | File ingestion daemon |

### 8.2.3 Dockerfile Examples

**Backend Dockerfile:**
```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile

COPY . .
RUN bun run build

# Production stage
FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine

WORKDIR /app

# Add non-root user
RUN addgroup -S mms && adduser -S mms -G mms
USER mms

COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./

ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=8000

EXPOSE 8000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8000/health || exit 1

CMD ["bun", "run", "dist/server.js"]
```

**Optimizer Dockerfile:**
```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS builder

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
  build-essential \
  cmake \
  git \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install OR-Tools
RUN git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/google/or-tools.git /or-tools
WORKDIR /or-tools
RUN cmake -S . -B build -DBUILD_DEPS=ON
RUN cmake --build build --config Release -j$(nproc)

# Build optimizer
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=/or-tools/build
RUN cmake --build build --config Release

# Production stage
FROM debian:bookworm-slim

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
  libstdc++6 \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Add non-root user
RUN useradd -r -s /bin/false mms
USER mms

WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/build/mms-optimizer .

ENTRYPOINT ["./mms-optimizer"]
```

## 8.3 Kubernetes Manifests

### 8.3.1 Backend Deployment

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: mms-backend
  namespace: mms

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the detailed summary for you.

---

## ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY: Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS)

Based on thorough analysis of **specs/002-architecture.md**, I've extracted the complete system architecture. Here's the detailed breakdown for implementation gap analysis:

---

### 1. HIGH-LEVEL SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

**Three-tier semantic canvas with layered data flows:**

```
CLIENT LAYER (React 18 + deck.gl)
έΦεέΦΑ Strategic View (12-year heatmaps, deck.gl WebGL)
έΦεέΦΑ Tactical View (12-week Gantt, Konva canvas)
έΦεέΦΑ Operational View (1-week details, React cards)
έΦΦέΦΑ Baseline/Drift Overlay (shadow comparison layer)
         έΗΥ
MIDDLEWARE (NGINX reverse proxy :80/:443)
         έΗΥ
APPLICATION LAYER
έΦεέΦΑ API Server (Bun + Hono, TypeScript, :8000)
έΦεέΦΑ WebSocket Hub (Real-time collaboration, :8001)
έΦΦέΦΑ Worker Layer (BullMQ-backed async jobs)
         έΗΥ
INFRASTRUCTURE LAYER
έΦεέΦΑ File Watcher (chokidar, daemon, :N/A)
έΦεέΦΑ C++ Optimizers (OR-Tools CP-SAT, stream-based IPC)
έΦΦέΦΑ Fallback Solver (TypeScript greedy algorithm)
         έΗΥ
DATA LAYER
έΦεέΦΑ PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB extension
έΦεέΦΑ Redis (cache + BullMQ store)
έΦεέΦΑ MinIO (report storage, file archive)
έΦΦέΦΑ Network Folder (/data/inbox/, /data/processed/, /data/quarantine/)
```

**Key Architectural Pattern:** Semantic zoom with real-time collaboration through WebSocket, constraint-driven optimization with explainability first.

---

### 2. COMPONENT RESPONSIBILITIES

#### 2.1 **Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)**
| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|----------------|
| **Timeline Rendering** | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom (0-16 levels) |
| **Strategic View** | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year (12-year horizon) |
| **Tactical View** | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule with weekly granularity |
| **Operational View** | Detail cards with technician, track, duration (daily granularity) |
| **Constraint Walls** | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
| **Drag-and-Drop** | Real-time PM rescheduling with live constraint feedback (red=invalid, green=valid) |
| **Man-hour Visualization** | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
| **Scenario Comparison** | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
| **Real-time Collaboration** | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |

**Tech Stack:** React 18, deck.gl (WebGL), Konva.js (HTML5), BlueprintJS v6, TypeScript strict mode

---

#### 2.2 **Backend API (Bun + Hono)**
| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| **REST API** | OpenAPI-spec endpoints at `/api/v1/*` |
| **WebSocket Hub** | Canvas updates + collaboration events |
| **Authentication** | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
| **Business Logic** | Orchestration of domain services |
| **Task Dispatch** | BullMQ job queuing for 8 worker types |
| **Optimizer IPC** | Stream-based communication (NDJSON protocol) with C++ binary |
| **Audit Logging** | Immutable change records with user attribution |

**Tech Stack:** Bun runtime, Hono.js, TypeScript, Drizzle ORM, OpenTelemetry traces, Pino structured logging

---

#### 2.3 **File Watcher Service**
| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| **Folder Monitoring** | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
| **File Validation** | Format, size (max 10MB), checksum verification |
| **Queue Dispatch** | BullMQ job creation for ingestion_worker |
| **Archive Management** | Move validated files to `/data/processed/` |
| **Quarantine** | Invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
| **Health Alerting** | Trigger alert when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |

**Concurrency:** Daemon (single instance), monitors in real-time

---

#### 2.4 **BullMQ Worker Ecosystem**

| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency | Period |
|--------|----------------|-------------|--------|
| **ingestion_worker** | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert mileage, handle meter swaps | 2 | On file arrival |
| **optimizer_worker** | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot | Daily (06:30, 22:00) |
| **tactical_scheduler** | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks (hard: 14d, soft: 90d) | 1 | Weekly Monday 06:30 |
| **strategic_planner** | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 | Weekly Monday 06:00 |
| **manhour_leveling_worker** | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 | Weekly Monday 07:00 |
| **drift_monitor_worker** | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting (┬▒5% mileage, ┬▒14d schedule) | 1 | Daily 22:00 |
| **report_worker** | Generate PDF/Excel reports (Puppeteer + ExcelJS) | 2 | On-demand |
| **notification_worker** | Send email alerts (SMTP, templated) | 3 | On-event |

---

#### 2.5 **C++ Optimizer Binary (OR-Tools CP-SAT)**
| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| **Constraint Solving** | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalty weights |
| **Explanation Generation** | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
| **Progress Streaming** | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve (5-second heartbeat) |
| **Metrics Emission** | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
| **Fallback Mode** | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out (default: 30s) |
| **Material Validation** | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |

**IPC Protocol:** NDJSON stdin/stdout with heartbeat monitoring (15-second timeout = 3 missed heartbeats)
